questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education does Wisconsin require for a salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Wisconsin requires 72 hours of REEB-approved pre-license education for salesperson licensure. Alternatively, candidates may complete 10 semester-hour credits in real estate or real estate law from an accredited institution.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed 10 semester credits in real estate law at a university 5 years ago. Can they use these credits for licensure?
questions.correct_answer: No, credits must be completed within 4 years
distractors: Yes, if the school is REEB-approved
questions.explanation: The alternative education path (10 semester credits) must be completed within 4 years of applying for a license. Credits older than 4 years do not satisfy the requirement.

questions.question_text: What is the alternative to completing 72 hours of REEB-approved pre-license education?
questions.correct_answer: 10 semester credits in real estate or real estate law
distractors: A bachelor's degree in any field
5 years of real estate work experience
questions.explanation: Wisconsin offers two paths: 72 hours of REEB-approved courses OR 10 semester-hour credits from an accredited institution in real estate or real estate law. Both must be completed within 4 years of application.

questions.question_text: Within how many years of applying must a candidate complete their pre-license education?
questions.explanation: Both the 72-hour course and the 10-semester-credit alternative must be completed within 4 years of submitting the license application to DSPS.

questions.question_text: Which topic is NOT typically covered in Wisconsin's 72-hour pre-license curriculum?
questions.explanation: The pre-license curriculum covers real estate law, property ownership, land description, title transfer, agency, contracts, financing, valuation, fair housing, environmental law, closings, and Wisconsin statutes. Commercial investment analysis is not a standard pre-license topic.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a Wisconsin real estate salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Wisconsin requires salesperson license applicants to be at least 18 years old. They must also have a high school diploma or GED and pass a background check.

questions.question_text: A license candidate has completed pre-license education. What exam must they pass?
distractors: The National Association of REALTORS exam
questions.explanation: Wisconsin contracts with Pearson VUE to develop and administer the real estate licensing examination. Candidates must achieve 75% on both the national and state portions.

questions.question_text: An applicant with a criminal record applies for a salesperson license. Which statement is correct?
questions.correct_answer: Only crimes substantially related to real estate may affect eligibility
distractors: Any criminal conviction is an automatic disqualifier
Criminal history is not considered at all
Only felonies within the last 5 years matter
questions.explanation: Wisconsin considers whether the conviction is substantially related to real estate practice. A background check is required, but not all convictions automatically disqualify an applicant.

questions.question_text: What passing score is required on each portion of the Wisconsin salesperson exam?
questions.explanation: Wisconsin requires a 75% passing score on both the national and state portions of the exam. The two portions are scored separately, and candidates must pass both to earn their license.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is required for a Wisconsin salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Wisconsin requires a high school diploma or GED, being at least 18, U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident status, completion of pre-license education, passing the exam, and a background check.

questions.question_text: How many total hours of continuing education must Wisconsin licensees complete per biennium?
questions.explanation: Wisconsin requires 18 hours of CE per two-year licensing period: 12 hours of mandatory courses plus 6 hours of elective courses. Electives must be Wisconsin-specific.

questions.question_text: Of the 18 CE hours required, how many must be mandatory versus elective courses?
questions.explanation: The 18-hour CE requirement breaks down into 12 mandatory hours and 6 elective hours. Elective courses must be Wisconsin-specific, covering WI forms, law, or administrative code.

questions.question_text: A salesperson takes an elective CE course on general negotiation skills with no Wisconsin content. Does this satisfy the elective requirement?
distractors: Yes, if the course is at least 3 hours
No, electives can only cover fair housing
questions.explanation: Wisconsin CE electives must include instruction on Wisconsin forms, Wisconsin law, Wisconsin administrative code, or a combination of these topics. A generic negotiation course would not qualify.

questions.question_text: A salesperson obtains their license on November 1 of an even-numbered year. Must they complete CE for that biennium?
questions.correct_answer: No, they are exempt for that biennium
distractors: Yes, they must complete all 18 hours
Yes, but only 9 hours are required
No, but they must complete 6 elective hours
questions.explanation: A salesperson licensed after October 1 of the even year of the biennium is exempt from CE for that biennium. Their first CE requirement begins the following biennium.

questions.question_text: What is the renewal deadline for Wisconsin real estate licenses?
questions.explanation: Wisconsin real estate licenses expire on December 14 of even-numbered years. The next renewal deadline is December 14, 2026. All 18 CE hours must be completed before this date.

questions.question_text: A licensed salesperson wants to open their own real estate firm. What additional license type is required?
questions.correct_answer: A broker license and a firm license
distractors: Only a firm license is needed
Only a broker license is needed
A managing agent license from DSPS
questions.explanation: To operate a real estate firm in Wisconsin, the individual needs both a broker license (requiring additional education and 2 years of salesperson experience) and a separate firm license for the business entity.

questions.question_text: How much is the biennial license renewal fee for a Wisconsin real estate salesperson?
questions.explanation: The biennial renewal fee for a Wisconsin real estate license is $60. A $25 late fee applies if the renewal is submitted after the December 14 deadline.

questions.question_text: To qualify for a broker license, how many years of salesperson experience are required?
questions.explanation: A broker license requires at least 2 years of full-time salesperson experience within the prior 5 years, plus additional REEB-approved education beyond the salesperson level and passing the broker exam.

questions.question_text: A salesperson fails to renew by December 14. What must they do to continue practicing?
questions.correct_answer: Renew late with the standard fee plus a $25 late fee
distractors: Retake the licensing exam before renewal
Complete an additional 18 hours of CE courses
Reapply as a new licensee from scratch
questions.explanation: Late renewals require the standard $60 fee plus a $25 late fee ($85 total). The license can be renewed late without retaking the exam, but the licensee should not practice while the license is expired.

questions.question_text: A salesperson submits their renewal 10 days after the December 14 deadline. What additional cost applies?
distractors: No additional fee for late renewal
questions.explanation: Late renewals incur a $25 fee in addition to the standard $60 renewal fee, making the total $85. Licensees should renew before December 14 of even-numbered years to avoid this penalty.
